title: Helianthemum oelandicum subsp. orientale

herb_names_common.name: eastern rockrose ( helianthemum oelandicum ssp. orientale )

what_description: Helianthemum oelandicum subsp. orientale is a taxonomic subdivision within the species Helianthemum oelandicum, belonging to the family Cistaceae. It is a perennial shrub with prostrate stems, 10-30 cm long, and 2-10 mm thick, bearing small, white to pink flowers, 1-2 cm in diameter, with five petals and numerous stamens. The leaves are linear to lanceolate, 1-3 cm long, and 1-5 mm wide, with a cuneate base and a serrated margin. The species exhibits a diploid chromosome number of 2n = 22, and is native to the Middle East and Eastern Europe. The plant grows in dry, rocky areas and rocky outcrops, often in full sun, at elevations of 500-2,000 meters.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Helianthemum oelandicum subsp. orientale are inflammation, wound healing, and skin irritation, which are reduced by its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. This plant also exhibits antibacterial and antifungal properties, making it effective against fungal infections and gastroenteritis, as well as infections caused by bacteria and other pathogens. In addition, it has been shown to alleviate digestive issues, such as diarrhea, and respiratory issues, including asthma, due to its antispasmodic and expectorant properties. Furthermore, it has been found to have antitumor and antiproliferative effects, which can help to prevent or treat certain types of cancer. Its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties also make it beneficial for treating skin ulcers, eczema, arthritis, rheumatism, and anxiety.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Helianthemum oelandicum subsp. orientale are primarily due to its antioxidant activity, which helps protect against oxidative stress and cell damage, while also contributing to its antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of various pathogens. The plant's anti-inflammatory effects are also notable, as it has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with various conditions. Furthermore, its analgesic properties make it a potential remedy for pain relief, and its antibacterial activity is particularly effective against certain Gram-positive bacteria. The bioactive compounds responsible for these properties include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are present in various parts of the plant, including the leaves, flowers, and roots. The combination of these compounds provides a rich source of medicinal properties, making Helianthemum oelandicum subsp. orientale a valuable resource for traditional medicine.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Helianthemum oelandicum subsp. orientale are flavonoids, specifically kaempferol and quercetin, which possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Phenolic acids, such as rosmarinic acid, are also present, exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. These constituents are responsible for the plant's ability to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The flavonoids in Helianthemum oelandicum subsp. orientale have been shown to have vasoprotective and anti-allergic effects, while the phenolic acids may contribute to its antimicrobial properties. The combination of these constituents contributes to the plant's overall healing potential.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Helianthemum oelandicum subsp. orientale are tea, tincture, infusion, salve, and decoction. Tea is prepared by infusing the leaves in hot water, typically used to treat digestive issues. Tincture is a concentrated liquid extract of the plant's leaves, made by soaking them in a solvent like ethanol, often used for its antimicrobial properties. Infusion and decoction are also liquid extracts of the plant's leaves and flowers, used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems and inflammation. A salve is a topical preparation made from the plant's leaves, used to treat skin issues such as wounds and eczema.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Helianthemum oelandicum subsp. orientale improperly are diarrhea, allergic reactions, vomiting, exacerbation of allergic reactions, and interactions with medications. These adverse effects can occur due to the plant's high content of iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which can cause gastrointestinal upset and allergic responses in sensitive individuals. Ingesting large quantities or using the plant for prolonged periods can lead to vomiting, diarrhea, and potentially life-threatening allergic reactions. Allergic reactions may worsen if the individual is already prone to allergies or has a history of anaphylaxis. Furthermore, Helianthemum oelandicum subsp. orientale may interact with medications such as blood thinners, diabetes medications, and certain antidepressants, which can lead to adverse effects or reduce the effectiveness of prescribed treatments.
